commit_msg: docs(readme): add changelog documenting qt thread safety fix
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Ledger
subtitle: He wrote down everything. The thread safety fix. The date. His name.
when_where: 🕐 2:24 PM on main, 13 March 2026. The kind of afternoon when documentation happens in the open—no dark corners, no late-night pushes. 📝 The README waited like a blank page in an interrogation room. Omkar sat down and started writing.
who_whom: 🔍 The changelog became his accuser. Qt threading issues—the kind of bug that doesn't just break code, it breaks the story you tell yourself about your craft. He was documenting not a fix, but a failure 💀 and worse, he was signing his name to it.
what_why: ⌨️ He typed out every detail: the problem, the solution, the date and time of his undoing. **Each line was a brick in his own confession.** The changelog would live in the repo forever, a public record of what went wrong and who knew about it. 📋 He wasn't hiding anymore—he was witnessing himself. 🧠 By documenting the crime, he made it real.
verdict: The man who confesses in writing has already decided he's guilty.
